extraction.one_line_summary: GROENEWEGEN Dylan won from a disrupted sprint after crosswinds split the field throughout the stage, taking yellow with 10 bonus seconds.
tactical_patterns: Sky used ROWE Luke to deliver KWIATKOWSKI Michał into late intermediate sprint for bonus seconds, not stage win tactics.
KWIATKOWSKI collected 6 total bonus seconds via intermediate sprints, revealing Sky prioritized GC placement over sprint stage control.
GILBERT Philippe attacked inside 3km from flattish finish, signaling desperation after missing earlier echelon splits.
Jumbo-Visma leadout placed GROENEWEGEN perfectly on right side while COLBRELLI Sonny launched too early from left.

rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: positive: third win of early season shows exceptional February-March form, already sharper than Tour de France rivals
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: negative: crashed out in Stage 1, second consecutive season derailed by early injury in first race back
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: negative: defending champion dropped by front group in crosswinds, lost 60 seconds revealing poor early season condition
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: second place despite surviving echelons suggests superior bike handling to pure sprinters who got eliminated
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michał: neutral: Sky deployed him for bonus second hunting not sprint assist, confirming GC ambitions not domestique role
rider_signals.COLBRELLI Sonny: negative: jumped first but faded, poor sprint timing cost certain top-3 after surviving selection
rider_signals.GILBERT Philippe: neutral: late solo attack from echelon survivor group shows good legs but poor tactical awareness
